name: Mars 3
first: First spacecraft to soft-land on Mars
description: The Soviet Union's audacious double-mission to Mars in 1971 — Mars 2 and Mars 3 launched 9 days apart. Mars 2's lander crashed; Mars 3's lander achieved the first soft-landing on Mars but transmitted for only ~110 seconds before signal loss, possibly due to the global dust storm raging at the time. The orbiter operated for 8 months, returning hundreds of images and atmospheric measurements. Despite the brief surface mission, Mars 3 demonstrated that the descent and landing problem on Mars was solvable.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Proton-K with Blok-D upper stage from Baikonur.

events.label: ORBITER MOI
events.note: Orbiter captures into Mars orbit during global dust storm.

events.label: LANDER SEPARATION
events.note: Lander separates and begins atmospheric entry.

events.label: SURFACE CONTACT
events.note: First soft-landing on Mars; transmission begins.

events.label: SIGNAL LOSS
events.note: After ~110 seconds, signal terminated permanently. Cause unconfirmed; dust storm leading suspect.
